"Our SAN infrastructure open book easy read, but next impossible duplicate," says Jason Silverglate, CEO and chief architect the company's storage solution. Silverglate says that his SAN was designed deliver performance with high-level reliability. The SAN's design includes redundant head nodes with redundant 10Gbps cards and redundant SAS 6Gbps wide internal SAS paths. The end result two highly capable volumes that can achieve extremely high read and write speeds.
Jason remarks, "on Volume are able achieve 1600MB/s read and 1300MB/s write. Mind you, Volume1 our low performance Volume... Volume which our SSD volume can achieve 250,000 IOPS and can easily max out few 10Gbps circuits."
That speed makes all the difference when comes cloud purposed storage and that what Jason and his team are counting on. Earlier this year, DedicatedNOW partnered with OnApp launch managed private cloud solution. The DedicatedNOW private cloud solution allows customers configure and deploy scalable, redundant hypervisors onto the SAN, connected through redundant switch stack via multiple gigabit connections both the Internet and The Company's own internal SAN network.
DedicatedNOW, provides managed dedicated servers and complex hosting solutions customers nearly 100 countries. More information about DedicatedNOW's private cloud and SAN technology available their website
For the past seven years, Jason and his team have been building and maintaining complex dedicated server environments for ecommerce companies. "We've learned allot about supporting and maintaining clusters and load balanced setups during that time," says Silverglate. "For starters, scalability problem with physical clusters. You can't expand resources moment's notice. Servers need brought down for upgrades, planning needs into making these kinds changes." With the expansion cloud technology, many the constraints within the physical world are done away with.
Jason and his team have been developing their cloud technology for the past two years from the Company's New Jersey based datacenter. cornerstone that development has been improving some the downfalls complex hosting. Jason commented the benefits the shift virtualization, "when move complex setup virtual environment, lose those physical constraints. can scale horizontally and vertically and for less money".
Ditlev Bredahl, CEO OnApp, has worked with FortressITX throughout the development their cloud. Bredahl remarks, "I'm impressed the significant investments Fortress has made into their SAN infrastructure. When speaking with our own clients, often recommend that they take look the storage technology that Jason and FortressITX have designed, before investing their own." Silverglate claims that the Company's SAN storage capable performance that most never thought possible virtualized environment, due its unique architecture.
Jason will presenting his ideas virtualization the OnApp booth (#313) Tuesday, Aug. 9th 11:45AM and again Wednesday, Aug. 10th 12:45PM.
"This merger brings together unique combination talents between DedicatedNOW and Solar VPS. Our new company has vast experience with Windows hosting, virtualization, dedicated and managed hosting," said Ross Brouse, former CEO SolarVPS, who will now serve COO the combined company. Although there change management existing server locations, the merger will provide benefits new and existing customers both companies. DedicatedNOW customers now have access world-class Windows web hosting support and virtualization expertise. The new company's datacenter has multi Gigabit bandwidth network accessing all key tier providers, while hosting routers, load balancers, firewalls, Internet servers and switches and accommodating large and small clients.
"I'm excited about the new opportunities that will open result this move. Both DedicatedNOW and SolarVPS customers will have access more staff, better resources and wider product selection. The merger makes both brands stronger, more marketable and sets the foundation for the growth our cloud hosting division," says Jason Silverglate, CEO.

original text:
Hydra module that can load-balance between symmetric Dataworks Enterprise sources.
used spread the load client requests amoungst multiple sources that can all potentially provide the same data.
Hydra commonly used where the underlying data source suffers has some form bottle neck associated with obtaining the data, and where this can aliviated having multiple handlers access the data.
For example, when the number requests made the Global Topic Handler becomes very high the Global Topic Server which connects may drop updates (with notification) prevent buffer over flows. One solution this run multiple GTHandlers against one more Global Topic servers each mounting seperate sources. Hydra may then used combine these multiple but logically equivalent sources into one, spreading the requests for GlobalTopic data amoungst each base source.
Hydra has been standard Dataworks Enterprise component since Dataworks Enterprise release 1.0.0.137.
When the client the Hydra's out-going source makes request, the hydra applies the request one its available in-coming heads (input sources). does this choosing the 'least-loaded' head calculated the basis the number currently active requests each head.
Hydra also supports single backup head (or input source) which will direct requests when, and only when, all other heads are unavailable. this situation will return requests back onto the first normal head that becomes available again.
Hydra can also configured place limit the maximum number requests that will apply given head. this maximum reached for all available heads then further requests will given RTStale status and will then queued until such time head becomes available that can process the request.
All Configuration performed using the Hydra.USR file. For specific details please consult the installed configuration file.
Hydra presents just one outpur source which has the behaviour the underlying input sources.
All the input sources should all equally capable servicing any client request.
Hydra does not rebalance requests once they are made. Requests are always applied the 'least-loaded' head the time that the request made and remain that had until the request cancelled.
present Hydra sends client request the least-loaded head calculated the basis the number currently active requests each head. forthcoming version will able use alternative, source-specific algorithim interogating each source for its relative loading factor using well-known instrument.

The SSRPC E-1071-623 ideally suited switching and monitoring hydraulic and pneumatic solenoid valves and magnetic brakes. These actuators with coils are often fitted machinery and plants steel production and processing (e.g. rolling mills). Type E-1071-623 complies with the requirements the new European Machinery Directive 2006/42/EC and the associated standard IEC 60204-1 "Safety Machinery Electrical Equipment Machines". Paragraph 9.4.3.1 "Earth Fault" the latter stipulates binding requirements how ensure double pole switching actuators and protection these components and their cables ungrounded systems (IT systems).
The E-1071-623 double pole device designed for rail mounting and compensates different cable lengths and cross sections and has operated with higher rated voltage (DC 28...60 than that the loads (DC V). Electronic control the load current allows supply each actuator with the required pull-in power.
During hold duty the load current reduced lower value. Current rating adjustment between 0.1...3.1 between 10...310 allows individual settings. The double electronic switching output and ensures physical disconnection supply accordance with the standard. Integral optocouplers activate the PLC and provide visual status and failure indication.
Example: The integral electronic control unit verifies whether there indeed solenoid valve the load circuit. Wire break detected even only the valve plug with LED wiring connected.
